widgets_values: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

Egyptian royal in blue-and-gold headdress and high collar, white dress with golden embroidery and armbands, desert, robot soldiers in formation left and right. She walks steadily forward, head held level and gaze fixed ahead—no dipping or lowering of the head. The camera performs a single, smooth push-in only: starting in a wider shot of her, the robots, and the desert, it moves steadily forward until she is in a medium or medium-close frame, then holds. She stops, posture and head still upright, and says: “The old gods are silent. I am not.” Robot soldiers shift or march in place; sand and fabric move with the wind. No pull-back; the only camera move is the continuous push-in.

Raylight uses **FSDP2** to shard the model across GPUs.

In rare cases, a memory leak may occur when switching from one LoRA to another while using FSDP. However, in most tests this issue does not appear, even with the same Docker setup....

If memory leak cases continue to grow, I will force a reinitialization of the Ray worker.

In the meantime, if you encounter this issue, please restart Comfy.
Open Issue on [Raylight Issue](https://github.com/komikndr/raylight/issues)

nodes.title: Rule of thumb
widgets_values:
Just jack up more ulysses per number of GPUs, and set ring only 1.

No need FSDP if all of your model can be fitted into individual card. Especially image model who has low sequence length.

For SDXL and SD1.5 only CFG parallel

nodes.title: Glosarry
widgets_values:
rank = id assigned to the GPU
world_size = number of GPUs

nodes.title: Introduction
widgets_values:
## Raylight Overview

As the name implies, **Raylight** uses Ray workers, called *Actors*.  
Each worker is its own Python process. While this allows splitting across multiple VRAMs,  
it does increase overall RAM usage.  

That said, RAM is generally much cheaper than VRAM, so this trade-off is acceptable in most cases.  

---

## More info: 

### [Raylight on GitHub](https://github.com/komikndr/raylight)
### [Raylight guide on Youtube](https://youtu.be/KQxrkJAV4eI?si=hAFY1rKD6Y2UHXqY)


## Supported Models

| Model               | USP | FSDP | CFG |
|---------------------|-----|------|-----|
| Wan2.X 14B T2V      | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Wan2.X 14B I2V      | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Wan2.1 1.3B T2V     | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Wan2.2 5B TI2V      | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Flux Dev            | ✅  | ✅   | ❌  |
| Flux Konteks        | ✅  | ✅   | ❌  |
| Flux Krea           | ✅  | ✅   | ❌  |
| Flux 2              | ✅  | ✅   | ❌  |
| Chroma              | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Chroma Radiance     | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Qwen Image/Edit     | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Z Image             | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Lumina 2            | ✅  | ✅   | ✅  |
| Hunyuan Video       | ✅  | ❌   | ❌  |
| Hunyuan 1.5         | ✅  | ❌   | ❌  |
| SD1.5 (UNet)        | ❌  | ❌   | ✅  |
| SDXL (UNet)         | ❌  | ❌   | ✅  |

**Legend:**
- ✅ = Supported  
- ❌ = Not supported  

**Notes:**
- Non standard Wan variant (Phantom, S2V, etc...) is not tested

## Scaled vs Non-Scaled Models

| Model       | USP | FSDP |
|-------------|-----|------|
| Non-Scaled  | ✅  | ✅   |
| Scaled      | ✅  | ⚠️ |

**Notes:**
- Scaled models use multiple dtypes inside their transformer blocks: typically **FP32** for scale, **FP16** for bias, and **FP8** for weights.
- Only Ada Lovelace and newer GPUs support **FP8 scaled FSDP2**.
